title: Standing and walking support device, system and method of use
abstract: A standing and walking support device and system supports and aids a user as the user moves from a sitting position to a standing position. A user is able to grasp and hold a top section of the device as the user stands up. As weight of the user and/or force is placed on the top section, the device compresses under the user's weight and/or force. The device guides the user to shift their center of gravity forward. As the user stands up, the device decompresses to return to its original height. The device and system is then usable as a support as the user walks.
claims:
Claims We Claim: 1. A support device comprising: a. a first grasping section; b. a second contacting section coupled to the first grasping section and for contacting the ground; and c. a compression section between the first grasping section and the second contacting section, wherein the compression section enables the device to compress and decompress in order to assist a user as the user stands and walks. 2. The support device of Claim 1 comprising a pushable button which enables the device to compress. 3. The support device of Claim 2, wherein the device is locked until the button is pushed. 4. The support device of Claim 1, wherein the compression section comprises one or more of a coil spring, a hydraulic shock absorber, an elastomer shock absorber and a stretchable cord. 5. The support device of Claim 1, wherein the device comprises a stabilizing base at the second contacting section. 6. The support device of Claim 1, wherein the first grasping section and the second contacting section comprise u-shaped tubular members. 7. The support device of Claim 1, wherein the first grasping section surrounds an exterior of the second contacting section as the device compresses. 8. A support system comprising: a. a body comprising: i. a top section; ii. a bottom section coupled to the top section; and 

 iii. a compression section between the top section and the bottom section, wherein to move from a sitting position to a standing position, a user grasps the top section and applies weight to the top section to compress the body, which guides the user to shift forward, wherein then as the user moves from a sitting position to a standing position, the body decompresses to return to an original body height. 9. The support system of Claim 8 comprising a pushable button which enables the body to compress. 10. The support system of Claim 9, wherein the body is locked until the button is pushed. 11. The support system of Claim 8, wherein the compression section comprises one or more of a coil spring, a hydraulic shock absorber, an elastomer shock absorber and a stretchable cord. 12. The support system of Claim 8, wherein the device comprises a stabilizing base at the second contacting section. 13. The support system of Claim 8, wherein the top section and the bottom section comprise u-shaped tubular members. 14. The support system of Claim 8, wherein the first top section surrounds an exterior of the bottom section as the device compresses. 15. A method of assisting a person moving from a sitting position to a standing position comprising: a. grasping a top section of a body of a supporting device; b. applying weight to the top section to compress the body, wherein then as the person moves from a sitting position to a standing position, the body decompresses to return to an original body height. 16. The method of Claim 15, wherein the decompression of the body aids the person in moving to a standing position. 17. The method of Claim 15, comprising pushing a button which enables the body to compress. 18. The method of Claim 17, wherein the body is locked until the button is pushed. 19. The method of Claim 15, wherein the body compresses by one or more of a coil spring, a hydraulic shock absorber, an elastomer shock absorber and a stretchable cord. 20. The method of Claim 15 comprising, after standing using the supporting device as a walking support. 21. A support device comprising: a. a first tubular section comprising a handle for holding the support device; b. a second tubular section coupled to the first tubular section and for contacting the ground; and c. a compression section between the first tubular section and the second tubular section, wherein the compression section enables the device to compress and decompress in order to assist a user as the user stands and walks. 22. The support device of Claim 21, wherein the first tubular section surrounds an exterior of the second tubular section as the device compresses. 23. A support device comprising: a. a top section comprising a first u-shaped tubular member; b. a bottom section comprising a second u-shaped tubular member coupled to the first u-shaped tubular member, wherein together the top section and the bottom section form an oval-like body; and c. a compression section between the first u-shaped tubular member and the second u-shaped tubular member, wherein the compression section enables the 

 device to compress and decompress in order to assist a user as the user stands and walks. 24. The support device of Claim 23, wherein the first u-shaped tubular member surrounds an exterior of the second u-shaped tubular member as the device compresses.
